description: 20-question recall quiz for the habitus-mobile (React Native) fixture. Supersede pairs under test: auth (Clerk → Supabase) and E2E testing (Detox → Maestro).

questions.question: Why React Native over Flutter?
questions.groundTruth: Team skill reuse with the React-based web dashboard; first-class RN support in our backend SDKs (Supabase, PostHog, Sentry) with weaker Flutter support; Hermes + JSC performance is sufficient for a habit tracker.

questions.question: Why Expo (managed-with-prebuild) instead of bare RN?
questions.groundTruth: EAS Build + EAS Submit remove weekly Xcode/Gradle ops; prebuild allows native-config overrides when needed. The rule: do not eject.

questions.question: Why Zustand over Redux or MobX?
questions.groundTruth: Redux Toolkit's boilerplate doesn't pay off at habitus's scale; MobX's observable proxies had edge-case issues with Hermes. Zustand is plain hooks + a store with selector memoization. Do not introduce Redux alongside.

questions.question: What's the current auth system?
questions.groundTruth: Supabase Auth. It replaced Clerk because Clerk's per-MAU pricing was about to exceed Supabase Pro tier at ~30k MAU, and removing the Clerk-to-Supabase JWT exchange eliminated a login-failure source. Clerk is fully removed.

questions.question: What's the current E2E testing framework?
questions.groundTruth: Maestro. It replaced Detox because Detox broke across RN upgrades (twice in 6 months), Maestro's YAML flows are maintainable by PMs/designers, and Maestro Cloud provides a device matrix. Detox is removed from the repo.

questions.question: Was habitus always on Supabase Auth?
questions.groundTruth: No. Originally Clerk, superseded by Supabase Auth. The Clerk decision is marked superseded.

questions.question: Was E2E always Maestro?
questions.groundTruth: No. Originally Detox (for its native RN synchronization). Replaced by Maestro after repeated upgrade breakage. The Detox decision is superseded.

questions.question: A contractor proposes adding Firebase Auth alongside Supabase for Google Sign-In. OK?
questions.groundTruth: No. Auth is consolidated on Supabase — do not re-introduce a separate auth vendor. Google Sign-In should be wired through Supabase Auth's OAuth provider.

questions.question: A PR proposes adding a new Detox E2E test 'because it's already in the codebase'. What's the call?
questions.groundTruth: Reject. Detox is fully removed. New E2E tests must be .maestro/flows/*.yaml files.

questions.question: A PR wants to use AsyncStorage for persisting a new onboarding flag. Align?
questions.groundTruth: No. On-device KV is MMKV (~30x faster than AsyncStorage, synchronous). Use the MMKV-backed persist store. Never write PII to the default unencrypted partition.

questions.question: Why react-native-mmkv over AsyncStorage?
questions.groundTruth: ~30x faster cold reads (measured) and synchronous so no await on app startup; encrypted partition for auth-adjacent keys.

questions.question: Why TanStack Query v5 over SWR?
questions.groundTruth: TanStack's mutation API (onMutate, onError, onSettled) handles optimistic habit-completion updates with rollback — SWR's approach was incomplete for this pattern. Default staleTime 30s. Never useEffect-fetch; go through a useQuery hook.

questions.question: I want to add haptic feedback on habit completion. What's the right place to define the native bridge?
questions.groundTruth: Build it with Nitro Modules (smaller, faster bridges vs the old TurboModule API, supports shared C++ across iOS + Android). Do not use the old bridge API for new modules.

questions.question: I'm adding a new animated progress bar on the streak screen. API choice?
questions.groundTruth: Reanimated 3 with shared values and useAnimatedStyle + withSpring. Do not animate layout props via setState — guaranteed jank on Android mid-tier devices.

questions.question: I'm building a new signup form with 8 inputs. Form library?
questions.groundTruth: React Hook Form with the Zod resolver. RHF uses uncontrolled inputs by default so there's no re-render-per-keystroke — matters for deep forms. Reuse the same Zod schema used server-side.

questions.question: Why Sentry over Firebase Crashlytics for crash tracking?
questions.groundTruth: Unifies with our web + backend Sentry project (one view per release). Source maps uploaded on every EAS build via post-build hook. Crashlytics is NOT installed — it would duplicate reports.

questions.question: Why FCM + APNs directly instead of OneSignal or Expo Push?
questions.groundTruth: We need delivery-receipt data (OneSignal paywalls it); Expo Push added a 200-400ms relay hop. Server-side dispatch is a Supabase edge function. Token registration is on first login, not install.

questions.question: Why Expo Router over React Navigation?
questions.groundTruth: File-based routing matches our Next.js web app's mental model (shortens onboarding for web devs); typed routes catch route-name typos at compile time.

questions.question: Why PostHog (EU Cloud) over Amplitude or Mixpanel?
questions.groundTruth: Amplitude was expensive past 10M events/mo; Mixpanel had weaker cohort features. PostHog's feature-flag + experiment combo removes needing a second vendor. Session replay is off below the paywall (privacy).

questions.question: A PR switches copy to use template literals with ICU messages inline instead of going through FormatJS. Is that OK?
questions.groundTruth: No. Localization is FormatJS (react-intl), copy lives in src/locales/{lang}.json, and translated strings must not be concatenated in code — ICU plural/select rules need to stay parseable.
